7:["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Understanding the Basics"}]
8:["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Supermarket Simulator by Nokta Games puts you in charge of a small grocery store. Your goal is simple: buy low, sell high, keep shelves stocked, and grow from a tiny corner shop into a retail empire. The game features both single-player and online co-op modes, with Steam Cloud saves so you never lose progress."}]
9:["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 leading-relaxed","children":"You interact with almost everything directly — grab boxes from delivery pallets, physically place items on shelves, operate the checkout register, and even chase down shoplifters. There is no traditional pause button; time keeps moving while you manage your store."}]
a:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"8","cy":"21","r":"1","key":"jimo8o"}],["circle",{"cx":"19","cy":"21","r":"1","key":"13723u"}],["path",{"d":"M2.05 2.05h2l2.66 12.42a2 2 0 0 0 2 1.58h9.78a2 2 0 0 0 1.95-1.57l1.65-7.43H5.12","key":"9zh506"}]],"className":"lucide-shopping-cart w-6 h-6 text-emerald-400"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Your First Day Checklist"}]]}],["$","ul",null,{"className":"space-y-3 text-zinc-300","children":[["$","li",null,{"className":"flex gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-emerald-400 font-bold","children":"1."}],"Accept the initial delivery and unpack boxes onto shelves. Empty shelves mean zero sales."]}],["$","li",null,{"className":"flex gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-emerald-400 font-bold","children":"2."}],"Set prices using the pricing gun. Start with 15-25% markup over wholesale cost."]}],["$","li",null,{"className":"flex gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-emerald-400 font-bold","children":"3."}],"Open the store by flipping the \"Open\" sign near the entrance."]}],["$","li",null,{"className":"flex gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-emerald-400 font-bold","children":"4."}],"Stay near the checkout register — customers will not wait forever."]}],["$","li",null,{"className":"flex gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-emerald-400 font-bold","children":"5."}],"Watch for the end-of-day summary to see profit, expenses, and customer count."]}]]}]]}]
b:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["line",{"x1":"12","x2":"12","y1":"2","y2":"22","key":"7eqyqh"}],["path",{"d":"M17 5H9.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 0 0 7h5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 0 7H6","key":"1b0p4s"}]],"className":"lucide-dollar-sign w-6 h-6 text-emerald-400"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Pricing for Beginners"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"One of the most common mistakes new players make is pricing too aggressively. If customers think your prices are too high, they will leave without buying. A safe starting rule is to set prices at 1.2x to 1.3x the cost you paid."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 leading-relaxed","children":"Pay attention to the customer feedback bubbles above their heads. Green smiley faces mean good prices; red angry faces mean you are overcharging. Adjust gradually and find the sweet spot for each product category."}]]}]
c:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M16 21v-2a4 4 0 0 0-4-4H6a4 4 0 0 0-4 4v2","key":"1yyitq"}],["path",{"d":"M16 3.128a4 4 0 0 1 0 7.744","key":"16gr8j"}],["path",{"d":"M22 21v-2a4 4 0 0 0-3-3.87","key":"kshegd"}],["circle",{"cx":"9","cy":"7","r":"4","key":"nufk8"}]],"className":"lucide-users w-6 h-6 text-emerald-400"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"When to Hire Your First Employee"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 leading-relaxed","children":"Do not rush to hire staff. In the early game, your own labor is free. Once you have at least 6-8 shelves and find yourself constantly running between restocking and checkout, consider hiring a cashier. Cashiers handle the register so you can focus on keeping shelves full. Wait until you have steady daily profits of at least $500 before adding staff."}]]}]

Spills and trash lower customer satisfaction. Grab the mop early and clean regularly."}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-lg bg-zinc-900 border border-zinc-800","children":["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 text-sm","children":"Watch for shoplifters — they appear as suspicious customers who grab items and head for the exit without paying. Confront them before they leave."}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-lg bg-zinc-900 border border-zinc-800","children":["$","p",null,{"className":"text-zinc-300 text-sm","children":"Expand shelf space before buying expensive products. More shelf slots = more revenue potential."}]}]]}]]}]
